steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage claims the CFRP harvester's ability to deliver ‘steady energy loads’ is an ‘absolute prerequisite’ for powering wireless communication devices piezoelectrically. A supporting finding must explain why steady output specifically is necessary for wireless devices.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Intermittent or unpredictable electrical supply undermines the efficacy of wireless communication devices.: directly explains why steady loads are a prerequisite — without them, wireless devices fail. ✓
  • Choice B — The near-constant kinetic vibration of a spacecraft makes it possible to power its wireless communication devices using only non-CFRP piezoelectric harvesters.: suggests the CFRP design is not uniquely necessary, weakening rather than supporting the claim. ✗
  • Choice C — The CFRP electrode is incompatible with most wireless communication devices.: contradicts the passage's claim that the harvester can power such devices. ✗
  • Choice D — The high conductivity of the CFRP is what makes the energy output from a piezoelectric harvester sufficient for wireless communication devices.: shifts focus to conductivity, not the steady-load property the claim is built on. ✗
That matches Choice A — Intermittent or unpredictable electrical supply undermines the efficacy of wireless communication devices..
